body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` **Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` **Triggered by:** commit a9a95cda (PR #57974) by @RaviPidaparthi ## What Changed PR #57974 refactored `ResponseContext.GetInputItemsAsync()` to return `IReadOnlyList` (was `IReadOnlyList`), added a `resolveReferences` parameter to both `GetInputItemsAsync` and `GetInputTextAsync`, and introduced the new public `ItemExtensions.GetInputText(IEnumerable)` extension class. The README was partially updated (convenience generators section and EchoHandler snippet were refreshed) but the following new public APIs are not yet documented. ## Gaps Found - `ItemExtensions.GetInputText(this IEnumerable)` is a new public extension method (in `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses.Models`) that lets handlers extract text from any `IEnumerable` without going through `ResponseContext`. It is listed in the CHANGELOG but absent from the README Key concepts or Examples sections. - The `resolveReferences` parameter on `GetInputItemsAsync(bool resolveReferences = true, ...)` and `GetInputTextAsync(bool resolveReferences = true, ...)` is undocumented in the README. Users need to know that passing `resolveReferences: false` returns `ItemReferenceParam` entries unresolved (useful for forwarding raw references without a round-trip to the provider). - The README's Key concepts section describes `ResponseContext` only in a code comment (`// Returns IReadOnlyList`) but does not explain the semantic difference between `GetInputTextAsync` (text extraction shortcut) and `GetInputItemsAsync` (raw item access for multi-modal or structured input).
­¤ōÉ Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` ŌĆö edit In the **Key concepts** ŌåÆ **ResponseHandler** section, after the existing `ResponseEventStream` convenience generators table (the paragraph ending with links to Sample 3 and Sample 4), insert a new subsection: ```markdown ### ResponseContext ŌĆö input access `ResponseContext` exposes two complementary methods for reading the request's input items: | Method | Returns | Use when | |--------|---------|----------| | `GetInputTextAsync()` | `Task` | You only need the plain text from the input (most common) | | `GetInputItemsAsync()` | `Task>` | You need structured items (images, function results, references) | Both methods accept an optional `resolveReferences` parameter (default `true`). Pass `resolveReferences: false` to receive `ItemReferenceParam` entries without resolving them ŌĆö useful when forwarding raw references to an upstream model without a provider round-trip. The `GetInputText(this IEnumerable)` extension on `ItemExtensions` can extract text from any sequence of `Item` objects you have already fetched: ```C# Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_ItemExtensions_GetInputText // Assuming you retrieved items separately (e.g., combined history + input): var inputItems = await context.GetInputItemsAsync(cancellationToken: cancellationToken); var historyItems = await context.GetHistoryAsync(cancellationToken: cancellationToken) .ContinueWith(t => t.Result.Select(o => o as Item).Where(i => i != null).Cast()); var allText = inputItems.Concat(historyItems).GetInputText(); ``` ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets 1. **Write the sample code** in `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/tests/Snippets/ReadMeSnippets.cs` Add a new test method: ```csharp #region Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_ItemExtensions_GetInputText [Test] public async Task GetInputText_FromItemSequence() { // Combine input items and resolved history for text extraction. var context = new MockResponseContext("resp-123"); var inputItems = await context.GetInputItemsAsync(); var text = inputItems.GetInputText(); Console.WriteLine(text); } #endregion ``` 2. **README placeholder** is already shown above in Step 1 with the correct `` ```C# Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_ItemExtensions_GetInputText `` fence. Run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` after adding the test method. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README already contains all six required sections. No structural changes needed ŌĆö only the content additions described in Step 1. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver` ŌĆö only needed if the snippet test method adds new public types (it should not) 5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
title: [AgentServer] Docs: Document ItemExtensions.GetInputText and resolveReferences parameter in README
